question.primary_nl: Find the summary text and date for the incident with case number '203348T'.
nl_variants: What is the narrative summary and occurrence date for case number 203348T?

evidence.golden_sql: SELECT summary_text, date FROM incidents WHERE case_number = '203348T';
evidence.reasoning_steps: Performs a direct look-up on the primary key column case_number to fetch specific descriptive fields.

ground_truth.raw_value:
On Friday, March 23, 2007, at approximately 5:30 P.M., plainclothes deployment officers located a
vehicle that was possibly involved in a homicide. The deployment officers followed the vehicle until
uniformed patrol officers driving marked vehicles could conduct a traffic stop on the vehicle. Upon
arrival, uniformed officers attempted to stop the vehicle which then fled and a brief pursuit ensued. The
L/M/27 subject lost control of his vehicle and came to a stop at 4023 Bernal Drive. One uniformed
officer approached the subject’s vehicle and attempted to gain entry through the passenger door. The
subject fired one round, which fatally wounded the officer. Other officers fired their weapons striking
the subject.

The subject was injured and treated at Parkland Hospital.

Subject weapon was a .223 caliber rifle, 1 fired round, 29 live rounds. Marijuana and amphetamines
were also seized from the subject.

The subject was charged with Capital Murder, Aggravated Assault (four cases).

The officer was pronounced deceased from gunshot wounds at Parkland Hospital.

Four officers fired a combined 57 rounds. Involved Officers: W/M 11 years, 4 months service, W/M 7
years, 2 months service, W/M 6 years, 2 months service, W/M 7 years, 8 months service.
ground_truth.formatted_nl_answer: Case 203348T occurred on March 23, 2007. The incident involved officers following a vehicle suspected in a homicide to 4023 Bernal Drive; the subject fired a .223 caliber rifle, fatally wounding an officer, before being injured by return fire and charged with Capital Murder.

nl_answer_variants.text: Case 203348T (March 23, 2007): Subject fatally shot an officer with a rifle at 4023 Bernal Drive after a pursuit; subject was injured and charged with Capital Murder.

evidence.golden_sql: SELECT subject_weapon, COUNT(*), AVG(officer_count) FROM incidents WHERE date >= '2003-01-01' AND date <= '2015-12-31' GROUP BY subject_weapon ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC;

On Friday, June 27, 2008, at approximately 9:15 P.M., a uniformed officer was working in an off duty
capacity for a business at 2900 Wheatland Road. The officer reported an armed B/M subject entered
the business and forced him to the cash room. There he demanded employees open the cash room
door. An employee disrupted the robbery by initiating the fire alarm. The subject then directed the
officer to a field adjacent to the business. The officer reported hearing the hammer click back and
believing he was about to be shot by the subject, pushed the subject aside who fled on foot. The subject
stopped after a few feet and looked back at the officer, at which time the officer fired 3 times at the
subject missing him.

The offense was later unfounded.

The officer was terminated for a violation of departmental policy and charged with Aggravated Robbery.

Involved Officer: B/M/ 3 years, 10 months service.

On Sunday, August 12, 2007, at approximately 2:15 A.M., uniformed officers in a marked vehicles
responded to a shooting call at 200 S. Stemmons Freeway. Officers found two individuals who were
shot (one deceased/one in critical condition) as they arrived and they also observed a wrecked vehicle at
the scene. One officer was walking in the direction of the wrecked vehicle when a L/M/20 subject shot
the officer with a shotgun. The subject then shot himself with the shotgun committing suicide.

Subject weapon was a 12-gauge shotgun, 6 fired shells.

The officer was wounded by gunfire and transported to Methodist Central Hospital.

No officer fired their weapon.

a Knock and Talk operation at 9830 Chapel Creek Circle #107, on an apartment that was suspected of
selling drugs. As officers knocked on the door a Latin Male opened and then immediately slammed the
door shut. Officers entered the location when a L/M/18 subject fired a shotgun at the officers. Two
officers were struck.

The subject was not injured.

Subject weapon was a shotgun, one round was fired.

Subject was charged with two counts of Aggravated Assault Public Servant.

Two officers were injured.

No officers fired their weapons.

then jumped onto the hood to avoid injury. The security guard rolled off the hood
of the vehicle, which proceeded down the exit ramp. After the security guard
rolled off the hood, the officer fired one round at the vehicle.

reported injuries.

evidence.reasoning_steps: Applies a negative existence sub-conditional constraint using NOT EXISTS to isolate cases with a complete demographic exclusion ruleset.

What is the highest value of subject_count recorded among incidents between March 1, 2013 and May 30, 2013?

evidence.golden_sql: SELECT COALESCE(MAX(subject_count), 0) FROM incidents WHERE date > '2013-03-01' AND date <= '2013-05-30';
evidence.reasoning_steps: The query reviews the next 90 days after T_obs and extracts the maximum value found in the subject_count column.

ground_truth.formatted_nl_answer: Based on the history of multi-subject incidents before March 1, 2013, it is predicted that the maximum number of subjects involved in a single shooting incident over the next 90 days will be 1.

nl_answer_variants.text: Predicted max subject count: 1.
Projective analysis indicates that the peak subject count per incident for the 90-day forecast period following March 1, 2013, is expected to be 1.
Looking at the patterns, it's predicted that any shooting incident in the next 90 days will involve at most one person.

question.primary_nl: Based on historical geographic tracking before 2014, what will be the average latitude of shooting incidents during the 180 days after January 1, 2014?
nl_variants: Predict the mean latitude of OIS incidents over the 180-day period following January 1, 2014.
What is the average latitude for incidents between January 1, 2014 and June 30, 2014?

evidence.golden_sql: SELECT AVG(latitude) FROM incidents WHERE date > '2014-01-01' AND date <= '2014-06-30';
evidence.reasoning_steps: The query computes the mean value of the latitude column for incidents strictly within the designated future window.

ground_truth.formatted_nl_answer: Based on historical geographic tracking before 2014, it is predicted that the average latitude of shooting incidents during the 180 days after January 1, 2014, will be approximately 32.78.

nl_answer_variants.text: Predicted average latitude: 32.78.
Spatial forecasting models suggest a mean latitude of 32.7785 for officer-involved shootings in the 180 days following January 1, 2014.
Based on where these usually happen, we predict the average latitude for incidents in the next 180 days will be about 32.78.

question.primary_nl: Based on officer demographic profiles prior to 2012, will there be any shooting incidents involving at least one female officer ('F') within the next 365 days?
nl_variants: Predict if any female officer will be involved in a shooting incident within 365 days after January 1, 2012.
Will there be an incident between January 1, 2012 and January 1, 2013 where an involved officer has gender = 'F'?

evidence.golden_sql: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(*) > 0 THEN 'Yes' ELSE 'No' END FROM officers o JOIN incidents i ON o.case_number = i.case_number WHERE i.date > '2012-01-01' AND i.date <= '2013-01-01' AND o.gender = 'F';
evidence.reasoning_steps: The query joins the incidents and officers tables to check if any records in the future 365-day window match an officer with a gender code of 'F'.

ground_truth.formatted_nl_answer: Based on officer demographic profiles prior to 2012, it is predicted that there will be at least one shooting incident involving a female officer within the 365 days following January 1, 2012.

nl_answer_variants.text: Yes, female officer involvement is predicted in the next 365 days.
Predictive personnel modeling for the year following January 1, 2012, indicates a positive probability of an OIS incident involving at least one female officer.
Yes, based on the records, we expect to see at least one incident with a female officer involved over the next year.

question.primary_nl: Based on suspect profiles before January 1, 2013, what will be the total number of unique Black ('B') subjects involved in shooting incidents over the next 180 days?
nl_variants: Predict the number of unique Black subjects involved in shootings in the 180 days after January 1, 2013.
Count the unique full names of subjects with race 'B' involved in incidents between January 1, 2013 and June 30, 2013.

evidence.golden_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT s.full_name) FROM subjects s JOIN incidents i ON s.case_number = i.case_number WHERE i.date > '2013-01-01' AND i.date <= '2013-06-30' AND s.race = 'B';
evidence.reasoning_steps: The query tracks the unique count of Black subject names involved in shootings during the 180-day future prediction window.

ground_truth.formatted_nl_answer: Based on suspect profiles before January 1, 2013, it is predicted that 7 unique Black subjects will be involved in shooting incidents over the next 180 days.

nl_answer_variants.text: Predicted unique Black subjects (180 days): 7.
Extrapolating from demographic data prior to 2013, the projected unique subject count for Black individuals in the subsequent 180-day window is 7.
Looking at previous cases, it's predicted that 7 different Black subjects will be involved in incidents over the coming 180 days.

question.primary_nl: Based on location tracking patterns before June 1, 2012, will any officer-involved shooting happen at a location containing the word 'Avenue' within the next 90 days?
nl_variants: Predict if any incident between June 1, 2012 and August 30, 2012 will occur on an 'Avenue'.

evidence.golden_sql: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(*) > 0 THEN 'Yes' ELSE 'No' END FROM incidents WHERE date > '2012-06-01' AND date <= '2012-08-30' AND location LIKE '%Avenue%';

ground_truth.formatted_nl_answer: Based on location tracking patterns before June 1, 2012, it is predicted that no officer-involved shooting incidents will occur at a location containing the word 'Avenue' within the 90 days following June 1, 2012.

question.primary_nl: Based on coordinate logs up to May 1, 2015, what will be the minimum longitude value recorded among officer-involved shootings over the next 90 days?

question.primary_nl: Based on suspect race distributions up to January 1, 2014, how many records for Hispanic/Latino ('L') subjects will be registered across all shooting incidents over the next 365 days?
nl_variants: Predict the total count of Hispanic subject records involved in shootings in the year following January 1, 2014.
How many rows in the subjects table will have race = 'L' and belong to incidents between January 1, 2014 and January 1, 2015?

evidence.golden_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM subjects s JOIN incidents i ON s.case_number = i.case_number WHERE i.date > '2014-01-01' AND i.date <= '2015-01-01' AND s.race = 'L';
evidence.reasoning_steps: The query estimates the count of subject rows within the 1-year future window that possess a race parameter of 'L'.

ground_truth.formatted_nl_answer: Based on suspect race distributions up to January 1, 2014, it is predicted that 5 records for Hispanic/Latino subjects will be registered across all shooting incidents over the next 365 days.

nl_answer_variants.text: Predicted Hispanic/Latino subject records (1 year): 5.
Predictive demographic modeling for the 2014 calendar year estimates that five Hispanic/Latino subject entries will be added to the database.
It is predicted that we will see about 5 Hispanic or Latino subjects listed in shooting cases over the coming year.
